userText: CASIO has always been loyal customers.
corrected: CASIO has always had loyal customers. / I have always been a loyal CASIO customer.

issues.note: subject-object role confusion: CASIO (brand) cannot 'be' customers; use 'has had loyal customers' or reframe the subject
'has been' + plural noun 'customers' is structurally wrong; 'has been a loyal customer' (singular) or 'has had loyal customers'

suggestions: Clarify who is loyal to whom. 'CASIO has always had loyal customers' or 'I have always been a loyal CASIO customer.'
